rules: Prefer areas with high vegetation and green cover (parks/forests) for better air quality and environment.
Target areas with low slope or consistently flat terrain for easier running paths.
Proximity to residential clusters ensures high accessibility for the local population.
Avoid high-traffic roads to minimize noise pollution and safety risks; use buffer analysis to stay away from primary arterials.
Waterfront paths or areas near water bodies are highly preferred for aesthetic and cooling effects.
Ensure safe land use classifications, focusing on recreational, residential, or public park zones.
